channel.title: TopChinaCar News
channel.description: Chinese auto news, EVs, exports, policy and global market coverage from TopChinaCar.

item.title: Mazda EZ-60 / CX-6e Global Sales and Market Strategy Report (2026)
item.description: A full research report on Mazda EZ-60 / CX-6e global sales, covering China sales data, Europe and Australia launch strategy, overseas forecasts, manufacturing, supply chain and strategic risks.

item.title: Mazda EV Strategy Deep Research Report (2026)
item.description: A full research report on Mazda's EV strategy, covering the MX-30 lessons, Lean Asset Strategy, Changan Mazda EZ-6/EZ-60 track, Skyactiv EV platform delay, hybrid strategy, market layout and risks.

item.title: Leapmotor Lands in Mexico, BYD Posts 792K H1 Overseas, XPeng L03 Eyes 64 Markets
item.description: Leapmotor becomes first Chinese EV startup in North America via Stellantis, as BYD hits 792,256 overseas units in H1 2026 (+70.7%) and XPeng prepares a 64-market L03 global launch.

item.title: DELIVAN Deep Research: Chery Commercial Vehicle's Electric Van Export Playbook
item.description: A structured research brief on DELIVAN, Chery Commercial Vehicle's new electric light-commercial-vehicle brand, covering brand strategy, product technology, Europe entry, partners, manufacturing, tariffs and competition.

item.title: Chery Overseas Full Value Chain Layout: Deep Research Report (2026)
item.description: A structured research report on Chery Automobile’s overseas value-chain layout, covering R&D, manufacturing, brands, supply chain, channels, local talent, ESG, capital markets and strategic risks.

item.title: Chery Commercial Vehicle Overseas Sales Forecast: 2025-2030
item.description: A bilingual forecast model for Chery Commercial Vehicle's overseas sales path from roughly 40,000 units in 2026 to a 400,000-unit overseas target by 2030.

item.title: BYD vs Tesla: Global EV Leaders Deep Comparison Report (2026)
item.description: A full original research report comparing BYD and Tesla across sales, financials, batteries, autonomous driving, charging infrastructure, product portfolios, globalization, AI strategy, valuation and risks.

item.title: BYD Overseas Full Value Chain Layout: Deep Research Report (2026)
item.description: A full original research report on BYD’s overseas value-chain strategy, covering manufacturing, vertical integration, logistics, charging infrastructure, brands, key markets, R&D, supply chain and risks.

item.title: BYD Hits 175K Overseas in June, Korea Cuts Subsidies; XPeng Launches Mona L03 in Munich July 16
item.description: BYD posted a record 175,349 overseas deliveries in June (+94.7%) even as South Korea stripped it of EV subsidies, while XPeng chose Munich for its first-ever overseas world premiere and Leapmotor set a new monthly record of 93,376 units.

item.title: What EU Tariffs Mean for Chinese EV Makers
item.description: The EU's anti-subsidy duties were meant to slow Chinese EVs. Eighteen months on, the real effect is visible: not retreat, but localisation, PHEV substitution and a premium pivot.

item.title: Chinese Plug-in Hybrids: Why PHEVs Are Gaining Attention
item.description: Pure EVs made the headlines, but plug-in hybrids and EREVs are becoming China's stealth export weapon: no charging anxiety, no EV tariffs in Europe, and 1,000-plus kilometres of combined range.

item.title: Chinese Pickup Trucks: A Growing Export Segment
item.description: Pickups are the most brand-loyal segment in the markets Chinese automakers covet — and the segment where they are advancing fastest, led by GWM's Poer, BYD's Shark 6 and a wave of PHEV utes.

item.title: Changan's Overseas Strategy: From SUVs to Smart EVs
item.description: The quietest of China's big state-owned groups is running one of the most methodical globalization plays: Thai-built EVs for Europe, 22 overseas manufacturing bases, and a 1.5–1.8 million overseas target by 2030.

item.title: BYD vs Chery Overseas: Two Different Globalization Models
item.description: China's two biggest auto exporters got there by opposite routes — technology-led blitz versus market-led patience. Comparing them explains most of what is happening in China auto globalization.

item.title: BYD Hits 792K Overseas H1, XPeng Debuts in Munich, GAC Exports Surge 132%
item.description: Chinese automakers logged a historic overseas half-year in 2026: BYD's H1 exports hit 792,256 units (+70.7%), GAC surged 132%, and XPeng chose Munich—not Beijing—for the Mona L03 world premiere.

item.title: Why Chinese Automakers Are Expanding Faster Overseas
item.description: Price war at home, fatter margins abroad, and a closing tariff window: the three forces pushing every major Chinese automaker to accelerate globalization at the same time.

item.title: Why Chinese Automakers Are Building Overseas Plants
item.description: From Thailand to Hungary to Brazil, Chinese automakers are racing to manufacture where they sell. The logic: get inside the tariff wall before it rises further.

item.title: Geely's Global Brand Portfolio Explained
item.description: Volvo, Polestar, Lotus, Zeekr, Lynk & Co, Proton, the Galaxy line — no Chinese group globalized through ownership like Geely. Here is how the pieces fit, and why exports just hit a record.

item.title: Chinese EVs in Europe: Opportunity, Tariffs and Competition
item.description: Europe is the hardest market Chinese EV makers have entered — and the one they refuse to give up. Tariffs reshaped the strategy from exporting cars to building them inside the wall.

item.title: Chinese Cars in the Middle East: Why Demand Is Rising
item.description: The Gulf went from skeptical to one of the fastest-adopting regions for Chinese cars in five years. Petrol SUVs opened the door; EVs and flagship showrooms are walking through it.

item.title: Chinese Cars in Africa: SUVs, Pickups and Commercial Vehicles
item.description: Africa is the quietest front of China auto globalization — and possibly the most durable. The wedge is not EVs but affordable SUVs, working pickups and commercial vehicles, with local assembly starting to take root.

item.title: China's Car Exports: Why Global Markets Are Paying Attention
item.description: China has been the world's largest car exporter for three straight years, and the first half of 2026 shows the machine accelerating: record brand-level shipments, overseas plants multiplying, and exports becoming the industry's profit engine.

item.title: China Auto Export Data: What Global Dealers Should Watch
item.description: Wholesale vs retail, exports vs overseas sales, customs vs association data — China auto numbers confuse even professionals. A practical guide to reading them, and the five indicators that matter most.

item.title: Chery's Overseas Growth: Why Emerging Markets Matter
item.description: China's export champion for 23 straight years built its lead the unfashionable way: petrol SUVs, emerging markets, and local assembly — long before the EV wave. Now that base is funding a push into Europe.

item.title: BYD's Global Expansion Strategy: Key Markets to Watch
item.description: BYD went from exporting almost nothing in 2021 to targeting 1.5 million overseas sales in 2026. Its strategy rests on three pillars: ships, plants, and a product ladder from the Seagull to Yangwang.

item.title: BYD Hits 792K Overseas in H1, Geely Breaks 100K Monthly, XPeng Goes to Munich
item.description: BYD delivered 792,256 vehicles overseas in H1 2026 (+70.7%), Geely crossed 100K monthly exports for the first time, and XPeng will globally launch the Mona L03 in Munich on July 16 across 64 countries.

item.title: BYD, Geely Hit Export Records in June as XPeng Takes Mona L03 to 64 Markets
item.description: BYD's June overseas deliveries hit an all-time high of 175,349 units (+94.7%), Geely first crossed 100,000 monthly exports (+157%), and XPeng will globally launch the Mona L03 in Munich on July 16 across 64 markets.
